title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Cortlandt, NY | $900–$2,500 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Cortlandt, Westchester. Humane bat control for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Bats in your Cortlandt home or business require immediate professional attention. Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services throughout Cortlandt and Westchester County, using humane methods that comply with all local wildlife protection regulations. Our experienced technicians safely remove bats and seal entry points to prevent future infestations.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Cortlandt residents for years with specialized bat removal expertise, understanding the unique challenges of properties in this Hudson River community. Our licensed professionals use proven exclusion techniques, offer warranties on our work, and provide same-day emergency service to protect your family and property from bat-related health hazards.
localContext: Cortlandt's wooded residential areas, historic homes near Cortlandt Manor, and properties along the Hudson River are particularly susceptible to bat colonies seeking shelter in attics, chimneys, and wall voids. The town's mix of older estates and newer developments, along with proximity to Bear Mountain State Park and Blue Mountain Reservation, creates ideal conditions for bat activity, especially during spring and fall migration periods.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Cortlandt, NY?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Cortlandt typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your property's structure. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in Cortlandt and Westchester County?
faqs.answer: Yes, but bat removal must follow strict New York State guidelines. Bats are protected, so removal can only occur during specific times of year (typically avoiding maternity season from May to August). Our licensed professionals ensure all work complies with state and local wildlife regulations while using humane exclusion methods.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Cortlandt home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, visible bat droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, brown staining around potential entry holes, and seeing bats flying around your property at sunset. If you notice any of these signs, contact us immediately for a professional inspection.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive process includes a thorough inspection to locate all entry points, installation of one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, sealing all potential entry points with durable materials, attic cleanup and decontamination if needed, and a warranty to ensure bats don't return. The entire process typically takes 2-4 weeks depending on weather and bat activity.
